description: MERLIN Phase-0 benchmark on Jetson Orin Nano 8GB (MAXN_SUPER), mono RGB, single image. Teacher = facebook/map-anything-apache. fp32 weights + autocast(bf16).

student_untrained.note: latency only; untrained so accuracy is meaningless until distilled. Geometric encoders are unused for mono RGB and prunable (~168M effective).

teacher_int8_weight_only.scheme: torchao Int8WeightOnly, 176/184 linears, scale+pose heads protected
teacher_int8_weight_only.note: Near-lossless accuracy and -44% memory, but slower in PyTorch: no fused int8 GEMM on sm_87 (dequant to bf16). The int8 latency win requires a TensorRT int8 engine.

conclusions: C5 (>=5 FPS keyframe rate): MET. The 230M student hits 5.1 FPS at 378px and 8.0 FPS at 252px, untrained.
Latency win comes from the smaller student; memory + near-lossless accuracy win comes from INT8; INT8 latency win needs TensorRT.
Memory is comfortable (<3GB of 8GB) once loaded; uncompressed fp32 teacher (4.23GB) cannot load safely on the 8GB board (validates the compression thesis).
Next: distill student to teacher accuracy (needs real GPU-hours), build TensorRT INT8 engine, IMU scale+drift module.
